name: Quality Standards
text: The Supplier warrants that all Goods supplied under this Agreement shall: (a) conform strictly to the specifications, descriptions, and samples set out in Schedule 1; (b) be of merchantable quality and fit for the purpose for which such Goods are ordinarily used; (c) be free from defects in design, materials, and workmanship; (d) comply with all applicable Indian Standards (IS) specifications issued by the Bureau of Indian Standards and all other applicable laws, regulations, and governmental requirements in force in India; and (e) be properly labelled and packaged in accordance with the Legal Metrology Act, 2009 and applicable rules thereunder. The Supplier shall maintain a quality management system and shall permit the Buyer to conduct quality audits upon reasonable notice.

legal_basis.note: Implied conditions as to quality or fitness

invalid_if: Quality standards below applicable BIS specifications
Warranty of merchantability excluded without adequate disclosure
Non-compliance with Legal Metrology Act labelling requirements
source: Sale of Goods Act, 1930; Bureau of Indian Standards Act, 2016
title: Quality Standards
dispute_hotspots: Merchantable quality — implied condition under SGA 1930 S.16 cannot be excluded for consumer goods
BIS non-compliant goods — criminal liability under BIS Act 2016 S.17
Fitness for purpose — buyer's purpose must be communicated to invoke implied condition
Batch testing disputes — statistical sampling vs 100% inspection
